commit.message:
Complete replication with ITT extension, add figures, update CI

- Add Bloomberg float-adjusted rankings, fuzzy 2SLS, and reduced-form
  (ITT) estimators for the 1996-2024 Russell RD replication
- Add robustness checks: sharp vs fuzzy, donut hole, BH FDR, IO-tercile
  heterogeneity, clustered SEs, placebo cutoffs, pre-trend validity
- Extend analysis to 2015-2024 using ITT (2SLS underpowered post-banding)
- Rewrite README with full results, methodology, and figures
- Track output figures in files/ (previously gitignored)
- Remove dev artifacts from tracking (CLAUDE.md, AGENT_SUMMARY.md, etc.)
- Update CI to run tests + ruff lint only (notebook requires proprietary data)
- Fix all ruff lint errors across auxiliary/ and tests/
- 44 tests passing, ruff clean

commit.message:
docs: neural grounding gaps — 12 gaps documented with priority order

Category 1 (missing broadcasts): alpha idle, theta consolidation, delta
clearance — specified in oscillatory heartbeat spec but not emitting.

Category 2 (missing feedback loops): Gc learning, gain modulation,
efference comparison, generator balance — observe→record exists but
record→adjust doesn't close.

Category 3 (missing mesh-level): circadian rhythm, RSA measurement,
entrainment, amygdala fast path, global neuromodulation — require
cross-agent coordination not yet supported.

8 well-grounded mappings confirmed (cardiac conduction empirically
validated). Alpha heartbeat = keystone for most remaining gaps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Per-strategy V2 IP thresholds: N=201, PF=2.25, +81.6R

Each strategy now gets its own optimal IP threshold instead of one
global value. Thresholds set from per-strategy sweep data:

  HH_QUALITY:     0.79  (PF peaks here, dips at 0.75)
  EMA_FPIP_V3_B:  0.73  (PF rises as threshold drops, +51R)
  SP_V2_SIMPLE:   0.77  (PF peaks at 0.77, +34.7R)
  ORH_FBO_V2_B:   0.80  (elite sleeve, keep tight)
  ORH_FBO_V2_A:   0.78  (N grows without PF loss)
  BDR_V3_C:       0.74  (PF improves with more trades)
  EMA9_V5_C:      0.79  (insensitive, keep tight)
  BS_STRUCT:       0.80  (small N, keep tight)

Comparison:
  Uniform 0.80: N=182, PF=2.20, +71.2R, DD=6.6R
  Uniform 0.77: N=224, PF=1.94, +73.6R, DD=9.4R
  Per-strategy:  N=201, PF=2.25, +81.6R, DD=6.6R  ← best

Implementation: ip_v2_threshold_by_strategy dict in config, checked
in replay gate against raw active_score (bypasses global active_passed).

fix: Unify send-to button styles with heart/note/delete

Send to Mast, Send to Compass, and Send to Prompts buttons now use

background: none, border: none, padding: 2px, display: flex.
Removes the outlined pill button style that caused misalignment.
"Already sent" labels use consistent subtle italic text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix over-filtering that caused 16 hours of zero trades

Three misaligned settings were combining to block every entry:

1. edge.js strength labels misaligned with phase thresholds:
   EARLY phase allows 5% edge, but the GOOD label required 10%.
   So every EARLY-phase signal was labelled OPTIONAL, then killed
   by minTradeStrength=GOOD. Fix: GOOD now starts at 5%, STRONG at 10%.

2. minPtbDeltaUsd=10 blocked all trades near window open:
   BTC must be $10 from the opening price before a bet is allowed,
   but during the first 3-4 min it rarely moves that far.  Changed
   to 0: now just checks direction (BTC must be moving the right way).

3. entryDelayMinutes=4 left only 1 minute in EARLY phase (timeLeft=11):
   Reduced to 3, giving a 2-minute EARLY window (timeLeft=11-12) where
   5% edge is enough to fire a GOOD-strength trade.

Dashboard: filter status badge now shows the specific blocking reason
(e.g. 'delay: 2.3/3min elapsed') instead of generic 'Window closed'.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sovereign metadata: add SHA-256 integrity hashes + verification endpoint

Every published asset now includes a metadata_hash (SHA-256 of canonical JSON).
This hash can be committed on-chain via Kaspa DAG for immutable verification.

New endpoint: POST /verify — submit UAL + metadata, returns verified/tampered status.
Detects any modification to stored metadata by comparing SHA-256 hashes.

Architecture: sovereign-metadata (read layer) + Kaspa DAG (truth layer)
- Full metadata stored locally (private, sovereign)
- Hash commitment on-chain for immutable integrity proof
- Anyone can verify by hashing metadata and comparing to on-chain commitment

Tested: publish with hash, verify correct (true), verify tampered (detected)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sovereign metadata service: replaces DKG with self-hosted private store

Built our own metadata service instead of depending on OriginTrail's
proprietary DKG Edge Node (which requires authenticated repo access).

- sovereign-metadata/server.js: Node.js service on port 8900
  - POST /publish → store metadata, return UAL (did:assetmint:sovereign/...)
  - GET /get?ual= → retrieve by UAL
  - GET /info → service status (mode: private, sovereign: true)
  - GET /health → health check
  - GET /assets → list all stored assets
  - Storage: local JSON file (Docker volume)
  - Zero external dependencies

- docker-compose.yml: updated to use sovereign-metadata service
- dkg-bridge/src/publish.ts: wired to real HTTP POST (no more mock UALs)
- dkg-bridge/src/retrieve.ts: wired to real HTTP GET (no more null)

Verified: service running on localhost:8900, asset published and retrieved
with real UAL: did:assetmint:sovereign/d53ac7095c089f32

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(terminal): WebSocket protocol aligned to OpenAlgo v2 docs

Verified against OpenAlgo MCP searchDocumentation:

WebSocket protocol fixes:
- Auth: send { action: "authenticate", api_key: "..." } on connect
- Subscribe: { action: "subscribe", symbols: [...], mode: "LTP" }
  was: { action: "subscribe_ltp", instruments: [...] }
- Unsubscribe: { action: "unsubscribe", symbols: [...] }
- Tick parsing: extract data from nested { type: "market_data", data: {...} }
  was: reading symbol/ltp from root level
- Silently ignore system messages (auth responses, type/status/message fields)
- Pass API key through getWsService → WebSocketService constructor

REST API fix:
- ping: POST (was incorrectly changed to GET, OpenAlgo docs confirm POST)

Revert incorrect schema fix — evidence_workspace_sheets HAS all columns

The audit agent gave WRONG schema information. The actual table has:
run_id, source_file_id, sheet_name, blob_bucket, blob_bytes, checksum_sha256,
column_count, columns, column_profile — ALL EXIST.

My "fix" removed valid columns and added column_stats which DOESN'T exist,
breaking the normalize step completely.

Lesson: ALWAYS verify schema with execute_sql against actual prod DB,
never trust agent-provided schema listings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix multi-agent worktree conflict detection

Stele failed to detect file overwrites when multiple agents worked in
git worktrees because: (1) all instances shared ~/.stele/stele.db,
(2) absolute paths from different worktrees created separate document
records, so locks and version checks never triggered.

Four fixes:
- Project-root detection: walk up from CWD looking for .git
- Path normalization: convert to project-relative at every public API
  boundary so worktree paths unify to the same storage key
- Per-worktree storage: default to <project_root>/.stele/ instead of
  ~/.stele/, with STELE_STORAGE_DIR env var override
- Auto-lock + MCP agent_id: index_documents() auto-acquires locks when
  agent_id is set; MCP servers generate stele-{type}-{pid} and inject
  into write operations

commit.message:
fix(terminal): 5 bugs found during live OpenAlgo testing

Tested against Dhan Sandbox (OpenAlgo v2.0.0.1, local):

1. orderbook/positionbook/tradebook/holdings response format:
   OpenAlgo wraps lists: { orders: [...], statistics: {...} }
   post() unwraps json.data → hooks receive { orders: [...] }
   Fixed: extract nested array, fallback to raw for other brokers

2. expiry endpoint requires instrumenttype parameter:
   Was sending: { symbol, exchange }
   Now sends: { symbol, exchange, instrumenttype: "options" }

3. connectionStore initialized empty — API calls had no key:
   Now reads from VITE_OPENALGO_HOST/API_KEY/WS_PORT env vars
   Setup wizard overrides these when user configures manually

4. api.ts getBase() returned full host URL in dev mode:
   Caused CORS errors (bypassed Vite proxy)
   Fixed: return "" in dev mode (use Vite proxy), full host in prod

5. WebSocket control messages (connected, subscribe_ack) logged as
   warnings — now silently accepted via action field check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ALL schema mismatches in evidence_workspace_sheets (#72)

* Fix: loadWorkspaceFromDb reads actual columns, not nonexistent 'manifest'

Root cause: the function assumed a 'manifest' jsonb column on evidence_workspaces
but the actual schema has file_inventory, dataset_profile, package_semantics,
template_profile as separate columns. This caused "Workspace not found" errors
in every child function (understand, author, critique-revise).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix ALL schema mismatches in evidence_workspace_sheets queries

Full audit found 3 broken queries against evidence_workspace_sheets:

1. INSERT (line 1184): Was writing 11 nonexistent columns (run_id,
   source_file_id, sheet_name, blob_bucket, blob_bytes, checksum_sha256,
   column_count, columns, column_profile, source_file_name, source_role).
   Fixed: only write workspace_id, sheet_key, blob_path, row_count,
   sample_rows, column_stats.

2. SELECT in loadSheetRows (line 1602): Was querying run_id (doesn't exist)
   and blob_bucket (doesn't exist). Fixed: workspace_id + blob_path only.
   Hardcoded bucket name "evidence-workspace-blobs" in storage URL.

3. SELECT in loadHydratedWorkspace (line 1644): Same issues. Fixed.

PostgREST silently ignored unknown columns on writes (with return=minimal),
but reads returned undefined for blob_bucket causing blob download failures
in child functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Revert to stable: disable streaming cards, no native card patches

The native streaming card approach (onStreamText + onStreamEnd patches)
caused persistent issues:
- Race condition: card creation async vs onStreamEnd timing
- Session lock: activeTasks not cleared, blocking subsequent messages
- Duplicate messages: both streaming card and fallback text sent

Reverted to the simplest stable approach:
- createStreamingCard() returns false immediately
- No onStreamText or onStreamEnd patches
- Bridge-manager sends complete response as regular text message
- No race conditions, no session lock issues, no duplicates

Trade-off: No real-time streaming text in Feishu (response appears
after Kiro finishes, not character by character). This matches the
behavior of other platforms (Discord, Telegram) and is reliable.

Native streaming in Feishu requires a full custom adapter (not patches
on claude-to-im's adapter). This is a future enhancement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: test type classification service with TDD (21 tests)

Classifies each TestArtifact as behavior/implementation/non_functional
using heuristic signal detection:

Behavior signals: UI selectors, page objects, component render, HTTP
status assertions, user-centric test names, navigation, user interactions

Implementation signals: direct function calls, jest.mock/spyOn,
internal state checks, technical names (validates, parses, calculates)

Non-functional signals: performance timing, security checks, a11y

Also adds calculateBehaviorScore() for coverage metrics per feature/layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make onStreamEnd fully non-blocking: remove await _createProm

The await on the card creation promise in onStreamEnd was still
blocking bridge-manager's activeTasks cleanup. Even though the
final card update was fire-and-forget, the creation promise await
could hang if the card POST request was slow.

Fix: Remove ALL await from onStreamEnd. If the card exists in
_nativeCards (already created), finalize with fire-and-forget
and return true. If not (still creating or failed), clean up
tracking state and return false (let bridge-manager send fallback).

This makes onStreamEnd completely synchronous — no async operations
that could block the bridge-manager cleanup loop.

The Step 6 rewrite renamed the health response key from
acestep_status to compose_status. Update all 6 references in
compose.js so Initialize, auto-start polling, and crashed/disabled
checks all read the correct key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix session stuck: fire-and-forget final card update in onStreamEnd

The previous await in onStreamEnd blocked bridge-manager from cleaning
up activeTasks, preventing subsequent messages from being processed.

Root cause: bridge-manager's flow is:
  1. converse() → acquires/releases session lock
  2. await onStreamEnd() ← this was blocking
  3. finally { activeTasks.delete() } ← never reached until onStreamEnd returns

Fix: Make the final _nativeUpdateCard call fire-and-forget (.catch(() => {})).
onStreamEnd now returns true immediately, allowing bridge-manager to:
- Skip duplicate fallback message ✅
- Clean up activeTasks promptly ✅
- Process subsequent messages without waiting ✅

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Wild Robot-style painterly post-processing pipeline

Two-pass pipeline inspired by The Wild Robot's NPR rendering:

Pass 1 — Kuwahara filter: smooths flat regions while preserving edges,
creating visible directional brush-stroke impressions. 4-quadrant
sampling with lowest-variance selection. Kernel size adapts to device
(3 mobile, 5 desktop, 8 high-end).

Pass 2 — Edge wobble + brush overlay + color grading (combined):
- Sobel edge detection with noise-displaced sampling for hand-drawn
  wobbly edges (soft darken, not hard black lines)
- Screen-space brush stroke texture modulation (directional sine waves)
- Paper grain overlay at different frequency
- Warm/cool color split: highlights pushed golden, shadows pushed
  blue-purple (classic impressionist palette)
- Mid-tone saturation boost for painterly vibrancy

All textures generated procedurally (no external assets).
Auto-detects mobile for quality scaling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix session lock: onStreamEnd returns false to allow bridge-manager cleanup

Returning true from onStreamEnd caused bridge-manager to skip some
cleanup that releases the session lock, preventing subsequent messages
from being processed.

Fix: onStreamEnd now returns false. This means bridge-manager will send
a fallback text message (duplicate of the streaming card content), but
the session lock is properly released. The tradeoff is:
- Streaming card shows real-time text updates ✅
- A duplicate text message appears after streaming completes ⚠️
- Session lock releases properly, allowing multiple messages ✅

The duplicate message is acceptable for now; a proper fix requires
understanding the bridge-manager's session lifecycle management.
